entry.title: Re: error when trying to connect to public cloud connecti...
entry.content:
Hi @tridwip,

Armin

entry.title: Re: How to create a value help for custom and released domain fixed values?
entry.content:
Hi all,

@crus_ch made a point but only works when Domain name and Data Element name are same. 

I made a change working for my solution. Both domain and data elements are identical and this works for me.

        DATA dataelem_name  TYPE sxco_ad_object_name  .
        
        FIND '_DM_' IN domain_name.
        IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
            dataelem_name = domain_name.
            REPLACE FIRST OCCURENCE OF '_DM_' IN dataelem_name WITH '_DE_'.
        ELSE.
            dataelem_name = domain_name.
        ENDIF.

        CAST cl_abap_elemdescr( cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_name( dataelem_name ) )->get_ddic_fixed_values(

entry.title: In the Create Material app the Material Base Unit Of Measure - "Number" UoM is not available.
entry.content:
We need to assign UoM - Number but same is not available in dropdown.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: In the Create Material app the Material Base Unit Of...
entry.content:
This is possible through creating Custom Units of Measure (UoM). To create a custom UoM, it's required to create a ISO Code first then the Unit of Measure. Please follow these steps in order to do so:

Creating ISO Code:

  1. Access your CBC System or Manage Your Solution and go to SSCUI / Configuration Activity 102179 - '2. Units of Measurement'.
  2. Click 'ISO Codes' tab.
  3. Click 'New Entries' tab.
  4. Enter ISO code, example '1' and ISO code text 'Number'.
  5. Hit 'Save'.

Creating Unit of Measure:

  1. Access your CBC System or Manage Your Solution and go to SSCUI / Configuration Activity 102179 - '2. Units of Measurement'.
  2. Click 'Unit of Measure' tab at the bottom.
  3. Click 'Create' tab.
  4. Here create Internal UoM, example 'ZNU', Commercial, example 'ZNU', and Technical, example 'ZNUM.
  5. Then the Unit of Measure Text, example 'Number'.
  6. Then Enter ISO code '1'.
  7. Hit 'Save'.

NOTE: When creating custom units of measure always put 'Z' in front as (Custom Z) they are considered as Customer Customizing and are not effected by the Upgrade processes.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/adding-custom-units-of-measure/qaq-p/12384649

You should be then able to use Number as a UoM.

entry.title: Re: UI5ers Buzz #57: Recently Used Values History for Smart Controls
entry.content:
How do we clear the Recently Used values? The Clear button in settings does NOT clear them, the recently used values still show up (for smartfields).

We need to clear them because some of the descriptions have changes and the Recently Used items are confusing users. We click the Clear Button, but the old descriptions still show up.

Can you help please?

entry.title: Re: SAP User Roles and SAP Ariba User Groups
entry.content: Prem_Tracz, Thank you for your response! This explanation makes sense and aligns with what I was attempting to understand about the process. If I understand your explanation correctly, this process would not apply to normal SAP Roles, but becomes applicable for projects that will include both SAP ERP and SAP Ariba and has users that need to be in both systems. The extended roles are created in SAP ERP and also in SAP Ariba and then the master data for the user data can be imported from the SAP ERP and populate the peer groups in SAP Ariba with the applicable users. Thank you!!

entry.title: Re: There was an application error uploading custom attachment.
entry.content: Hi, we have the same issue, did you get a resolution? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud # Freezing Line
entry.content:
Hi Yogeshwar,

Yes you can change the colour of the line (but only the colour, nothing else about it unfortunately). You would do this with custom css in your story. First, add a custom css class to your table. I have used 'custom' for example:

Then in the custom css for your story, add code as follows:

 

.custom .sap-custom-table-freeze-lines {
	border-color: #FF6600;
}

 

Cheers, Ivan.

entry.title: Performance issue with Monitor Capacity Utilization App
entry.content:
Hi All

We are experiencing a performance issue when trying to open the resource utilization chart from the main page of the app. we have to click "wait" more than twice to launch the resource utilization chart.

This delay only occurs the first time the app is launched; subsequent attempts do not experience this issue. I found note 2795711 but it cannot be implemented for our release. Could you please let me know if anyone has faced this issue? Thanks in advance

Br/Adnan

entry.content:
@alex_bundschuhis it possible to define a recipient for inbound EDI , via SOAP B2B web Service OrderRequest_In - Namespace /SDEDI, in such a way that a specific user can only see in the Message Monitoring Overview  Fiori App the inbound EDI orders for a specific sales organisation? 

I'm asking this because normally the sales organization is not something that the customer sends, therefore not present in the S/4 Inbound SOAP XML, and it is determined by entries in Configuration table EDSDC( transaction VOE2)

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
@markus_fugger_de Your question touches exactly what I meant by this statement:

Currently there's still the challenge that adoption by applications is quite low.

Afaik currently you can use it with custom BTP applications if they authenticate directly with IAS and not via Authorization and Trust Management service (XSUAA). In that case, only your custom application needs to propagate the desired corporate IdP (which you'll usually achieve by having an own approuter as part of the application). Documentation for direct usage of IAS with BTP applications: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/integrating-service-with-identity-service-of-sap-btp

For SAP Build Work Zone, which has the option for direct IAS authentication only, this is a known requirement and I think it's planned.

Most BTP applications currently don't integrate directly with IAS yet, so this option doesn't work for them so far. I'd be very happy to be able to support propagation also in the longer chain, from application over XSUAA to IAS. However, unfortunately I have no way to get it into the near-term development backlog at this point.

Proposal: Open an influence request on https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/ and then let's collect demand there.

entry.title: Data source for master form
entry.content:
Which data source is to be assigned to the master form template?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Data source for master form
entry.content:
Please use the data Source: FDP_OM_FORM_MASTER_SRV

For detailed guide please refer to the 1LQ Scope item set-up instruction of the Solution Scenario: Output Management (1LQ) section 4.3.1.1.2.1  Downloading Default Master Form Templates and 4.3.1.1.2.3  Uploading the Modified Template.

The standard Master form template is SOMU_FORM_MASTER_A4_E.

SAP Help Portal - Master Form Templates

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA

Output Management in Purchase Orders: Your Ultimate Guide for Configuration and Troubleshooting

entry.title: SAP - DMC wrong inspection point valuation for process order
entry.content:

Need help to identify where I am missing any setting for the valuation of inspection point correctly in Digital manufacturing . All MIC's actual results are out of range , hence the MIC valuation result is ' valuation out of range'(image attached) , however , the Inspection point must have 'REJECTED' valuation in this case but in Digital manufacturing  the valuation  for inspection point is showing  ACCEPTED (image attached) .

MIC valuation image Inspection point valuation Image 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: UI5ers Buzz #57: Recently Used Values History for Smart Controls
entry.content:
Found out what the problem was with clearing history. The users were clicking on the Clear My History button under User Activities menu:

But that only clears the list of recent apps they've used.

They need to click on the Clear My History button on the Input History Settings when inside an individual app:

It's confusing that the two clear buttons are named exactly the same, but do totally different things.

entry.title: Re: Issue data blending in Story using table
entry.content: Dear Giri,

entry.title: Re: Creating a popup with depending fields
entry.content:
Hi Sebastian,

sorry for the late reply. The only option that comes to my mind is the following (in the abstract entity):

 

@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{entity: { name: 'I_Field1VH', element: 'Field1' }, useForValidation: true }]

Field1: some_de;

@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{

entity : { name: 'I_Field2VH', element: 'Field2' },

additionalBinding: [{ localElement: 'Field1', element:'Field1', usage: #FILTER_AND_RESULT }]

}]

Field2: some_de;

----

I_Field1VH contains all entries possible for Field1 (key: Field1).

I_Field2VH contains all entries possible for Field2 (keys: Field1, Field2).

 

In that case, if he enters Field2 first, he will get all entries. But if he selects one, Field1 will get automatically updated (due to FILTER_AND_RESULT).

 

Regards,

Michael

entry.title: Next Generation SAP Enterprise Architect Learning Forum
entry.content:
Gain leading-edge insights into how SAP and other cutting-edge technologies can drive business value at an unprecedented pace

Register Here!

Are you ready to shape the future of enterprise architecture? Join us for a live broadcast of the Next Generation SAP Enterprise Architect Learning Forum, from February 25–27, 2025.

While the in-person portion of this event is full, we encourage you to register for complimentary access to the online broadcast to attend the plenary and track one sessions.

Here’s what you can expect:

  • Unpack SAP Business Unleashed for Enterprise Architects
  • Expert-led sessions: Learn from top-industry thought leaders and SAP experts
  • Immersive experiences: Dive deep into the latest trends and best practices
  • Exclusive content: Explore cutting-edge themes and develop essential human skills to excel in today’s dynamic landscape

Who should attend the online broadcast?

This event is tailored for:

  • Enterprise architects at any stage of their career
  • SAP professionals aspiring to transition into enterprise architecture
  • By registering, you will have access to all session recordings for playback.

Next Generation SAP Enterprise Architect Learning Forum – Livestream Experience

Date and Time:

  • Tuesday, February 25, 2025 | 8:50 a.m.–5:30 p.m. ET
  • Wednesday, February 26, 2025 | 8:50 a.m.–5:30 p.m. ET
  • Thursday, February 27, 2025 | 8:50 a.m.–1:30 p.m. ET

entry.title: Re: Source list error due to purchasing organization not responsible for plant
entry.content:
Hello Laksmipathi,

I have got same error however even from generate sourcelist i am not able create sourcelist what would be workaround and sap note is not working which is mentioned,

entry.title: Re: Assignment profile not picking up some users
entry.content:
Since you have said the missing users are within the same domains as users who are being included in the assignment profile, and they meet all the rules of the profile, I think the most likely answer is that your profile is paused.

This happens when someone looks at the profile, perhaps to check it, and instinctively clicks Save. But then they don't re-run the profile. Saving it and not running the profile leaves it in Paused status.

(This is intentional functionality, to allow admins to save partially updated rules & finish changes later.)

To un-pause the profile, we have found that we need to not only Execute the profile again but also Update as well (both menu options). It is this 2nd part of un-pausing the profile that can be missed, even when the admin knows that action is required after accidentally saving a non-existent "change."

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to change a numeric value to a percentage value i...
entry.content:
You could also just configure two separate measures. So have one calculated measure configured to show the value, and then another configured to show the value as a percentage. Then write a small script to place on a button or whatever input control you like that will toggle which measure is displayed in the numeric point chart.

Script to toggle would look something like:

// Define measure IDs
var measure1 = "77373615-5410-4254-3668-914452161766";
var measure2 = "19715775-8237-4549-3898-205003124717";

// Get the currently assigned measures
var currentMeasures = Chart_1.getMembers(Feed.ValueAxis);

// Check which measure is currently displayed and toggle
if (currentMeasures.includes(measure1)) {
    Chart_1.removeMember(Feed.ValueAxis, measure1);
    Chart_1.addMember(Feed.ValueAxis, measure2);
} else {
    Chart_1.removeMember(Feed.ValueAxis, measure2);
    Chart_1.addMember(Feed.ValueAxis, measure1);
}

Cheers, Ivan.

entry.title: Error importing Business Content: Integrated Financial Planning for SAP S/4HANA (1.34.0)
entry.content:
Hi,

i'm trying to import Business Content: Integrated Financial Planning for SAP S/4HANA (1.34.0). I solved most of the errors by importing only parts of it regarding the dependencies but the last 3 Stories just don't want to be imported:

SAP_FI_IFP_IM_Cross_EndToEndPlanning
SAP_FI_IFP_IM_Cross_LandingPage
SAP_FI_IFP_IM_Cross_SimulationCockpit

Error Message example:

Couldn't import object 'SAP_FI_IFP_IM_Cross_SimulationCockpit [FA184702F63BE21AD02FF6476C806D05]' : Error importing object ID: RESOURCE:t.A:FA184702F63BE21AD02FF6476C806D05.

Each of the stories throws an error message when i try to import it separately - all other Objects are already imported

Thanks

entry.title: Can ABAP CDS views have columns with data type BLOB that stores images ?
entry.content: I am aware that images can be stored in HANA database and accessed using HANA Calculation Views. But does that hold good for ABAP CDS views. I have images stored in a table in S4HANA system and need to display them over a live connection in SAC Report. My ABAP CDS view gives error when I include the column with BLOB data type. Not sure if this is a limitation of CDS. Any thoughts ?

entry.title: Hosting SAP Code jam events in Warsaw
entry.content:
Hi,

I would be happy if I could host again SAP CodeJam events in Warsaw.

If possible, let's start with:

  • ABAP Cloud & ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
  • Proposed time frame: end of March '25 - beginning of April '25. Exact dates to be agreed with a trainer.
  • Location: Warsaw, KMD Poland office.

Later (in June or September) I also would like to invite the following workshops:

  • Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and SAP generative AI Hub
  • SAP Fiori elements flexible programming model

But let's talk about it later.

Thank you in advance for your feedback

Bartek

entry.title: Re: How to Assign Ressource to the Project

entry.title: RFC function module Maximum number of GUI windows reached error
entry.content:
When I use the function, it returns successfully, but it gives an error that the maximum number of gui windows has been reached. It only gives when I use this function.

There is no such problem when I look at other systems.
CALL FUNCTION ... STARTING NEW TASK the same problem happened.

rdisp/max_alt_modes parameter 16 on the system.

  CALL FUNCTION '/SCWM/TO_CREATE_WHR'
        EXPORTING
          iv_process     = '2'
          iv_lgnum       = mv_lgnum
          iv_mve_hu      = abap_true
          iv_mve_mat     = space
          iv_mve_hu_mult = space
          iv_squit       = COND #( WHEN mv_internal EQ abap_false THEN abap_false ELSE abap_true )
          iv_to_init     = abap_true
          iv_bname       = sy-uname
          iv_wtcode      = space
          it_create_whr  = mt_pull[]
          iv_commit_work = abap_true
        IMPORTING
          ev_tanum       = lv_tanum
          et_ltap_vb     = lt_ltab_vb
          et_bapiret     = lt_return
          ev_severity    = lv_severity.

what is the solution? how can i do it?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Can ABAP CDS views have columns with data type BLOB that stores images ?
entry.content: What's the rror?

entry.title: Discover What’s New in SAP CX AI Toolkit | February 2025
entry.content:
Starting a new year, we often ask ourselves: "How can we better serve our customers?"

 

We've been listening to your feedback, and our February release of the SAP CX AI Toolkit directly addresses the real challenges you've shared with us.

 

The latest update brings practical improvements that tackle your day-to-day pain points – from making sense of scattered customer data to simplifying those repetitive tasks that eat up your team's valuable time.

 

What is SAP CX AI Toolkit?

SAP CX AI Toolkit brings Joule AI agents and Business AI capabilities to your SAP Sales, Service, and Commerce Cloud applications.

 

Part of SAP Business Suite, it combines your enterprise data including Business Data Cloud with purpose-built AI to make every team member more effective. From smart ticket routing to instant knowledge access, it delivers practical AI that works from day one in a trusted, secure environment

 

New Features at a Glance 

  • AI Agents: Add Digital workers to your team to unlock higher productivity and efficiency gains
  • Shopping Assistant (Pilot): Enable your customers to easily discover intended, relevant products
  • Commerce Intelligence: Add AI-powered image overlays, summarize reviews, and extract product details from images
  • AI Tool Builder Enhancements:  Analyze opportunities, cases, and accounts together in one prompt to spot business patterns

Let's explore what's new and how these features can benefit your organization.

 

Joule Agents for CX Join Your Team

The star of this release is our new suite of autonomous Joule agents that work alongside your teams. They work behind the scenes with your SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud applications to enhance your workflows.

 

Bosch Power Tools is a great example of how one agent can replace hundreds of workflow rules: See their story

 

If you're wondering what this means in practice, here's a real scenario:

A customer submits a support ticket about a failed login attempt. Within seconds, our Case Classification Agent reads it, understands it's an account access issue with high priority, and routes it to your identity management team. Meanwhile, the Q&A Agent is already pulling relevant solutions from your knowledge base, and once resolved, the Knowledge Base Article Agent will automatically create documentation to help solve similar issues in the future.

This is just one example of how these agents work together to streamline your operations. Let's dive into what they can do:

 

Case Classification Agent

 

How It Works

When a customer submits a service request, the agent automatically:

  • Reads and comprehends the content using natural language understanding
  • Identifies the type of issue and its urgency
  • Determines the most appropriate team based on the content
  • Sets the correct details like product, language or whatever else is important to you
  • Updates relevant ticket fields to ensure proper handling

 

Real-World Example

Let's say a customer submits a ticket about a failed login attempt:

  1. The agent recognizes this as an account access issue
  2. Identifies it as high priority due to its impact on user access
  3. Routes it directly to the identity management team
  4. Updates the ticket category, priority, and assignment fields
  5. All of this happens in seconds, without manual intervention

 

Learn more in the SAP Help User Guide

 

Knowledge Creation Agent

 

This agent turns your team's case resolutions into valuable knowledge base content, automatically capturing and sharing expertise across your organization.

 

How It Works

When a case is resolved, the agent:

  • Analyzes the solution provided
  • Extracts key information and steps
  • Creates a structured knowledge article
  • Adds relevant tags and categories
  • Publishes a draft back to the case for review and add to your knowledge base

 

Real-World Example

After your team resolves a complex product configuration issue:

  1. The agent reviews the resolution steps
  2. Creates a clear, structured article
  3. Includes troubleshooting steps and solution
  4. Tags it with relevant products and categories
  5. Makes it available for future reference

 

Learn more in the SAP Help User Guide

 

Q&A Agent

 

The Q&A Agent transforms how your organization accesses and utilizes internal knowledge. Instead of searching through multiple systems, users can ask questions naturally and receive accurate, sourced answers instantly.

 

How It Works

When someone asks a question, the agent:

  • Understands the intent and context of the query
  • Searches across configured knowledge sources
  • Evaluates potential answers for relevance
  • Provides responses with source references

 

Real-World Example

When a sales representative needs pricing information:

  1. It identifies the question "What are the pricing tiers for our enterprise cloud solution?"
  2. The agent analyzes the question to understand:
    • The specific product line being asked about
    • The need for pricing structure information
    • The enterprise-level context
  3. It searches across relevant sources like:
    • Price sheets
    • Policy documents
    • Previous similar queries
  4. The agent provides a structured response:
    • Clear tier breakdowns
    • Source details

 

Learn more in the SAP Help User Guide

 

 

Simplify Intent to Cart on Your e-Commerce Storefront

 

We're also excited to announce the pilot of our Shopping Assistant. Early adopters are seeing how it transforms basic product searches into meaningful conversations that help customers find exactly what they need.

A customer looking for a laptop doesn't just get a list of products - they get an intelligent assistant that asks about their needs, understands their preferences, and guides them to the perfect match.

 

For more details, check out the comprehensive user guide in SAP Help

 

Power to the Content Creators

For your commerce teams, we're introducing tools that make content creation and management more efficient in how you manage visual content, analyze customer feedback, and extract information from images.

Let's explore these powerful new capabilities.

 

AI Image Generation: Transform Your Product Presentation

 

What is it?

Advanced imaging features that simplify the creation of customized visual media for your commerce presence.

 

Key Features

  • Image Overlays: Add text and graphics to product images with AI-assisted placement
  • Publishing Support: Automate image publishing to SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Brand Enhancement Tools: Create compelling product stories through visual elements

 

How it works

The system provides an intuitive interface for adding overlays to product images, with AI helping to optimize placement. Changes can be published to SAP Commerce Cloud, streamlining your visual content workflow.

 

Key Benefits

  • Faster content creation and updates
  • Consistent brand presentation
  • Easy publishing workflow
  • Enhanced visual storytelling

 

 

Commerce Review AI Tool: Unlock Customer Insights

 

What is it?

A powerful tool that uses generative AI to analyze and leverage customer reviews from Commerce Cloud.

Key Features

  • AI-powered review summarization for specific products
  • Sentiment analysis with keyword highlighting
  • Integration with Ask about this Product AI Tool
  • Comprehensive review analysis through UI or API

 

How it works

The system processes customer reviews from your Commerce Cloud platform, analyzing content for sentiment and key themes. This information can be accessed through the toolkit interface or integrated into your product pages.

Key Benefits

  • Deeper customer insight
  • Automated feedback analysis
  • Enhanced product understanding
  • Data-driven decision making

 

Visual Information Extraction: Smart Image Analysis

 

What is it?

An advanced system that extracts and utilizes visual information to provide product recommendations and insights.

 

Key Features

  • Natural language prompts for visual analysis
  • Automated text extraction (e.g., model numbers)
  • Business-specific insight generation
  • API integration for storefront implementation
  • User intent-based recommendations

 

How it works

Users can analyze images using natural language prompts, extracting specific business information and generating recommendations. The system can identify text, product attributes, and other relevant details, making this information available through APIs for storefront integration.

 

Key Benefits

  • Enhanced product discovery
  • Automated attribute extraction
  • Improved accuracy for visual search
  • Seamless storefront integration

 

Custom AI Tool Builder: Power for Complex Business Scenarios

One of our most significant enhancements is the expansion of the Custom AI Tool Builder's capabilities. We've heard from many of you that you need to analyze multiple related records to get a complete business picture. Now you can.

 

 

The enhanced builder now lets you reference multiple CRM records in your custom AI Tool prompts. Here's what that means in practice:

  • Account-Level Analysis: Create tools that examine all opportunities, cases and more linked to an account, giving you a comprehensive view of customer engagement
  • Case Pattern Recognition: Build tools that analyze multiple escalated cases to identify common patterns or issues
  • Competitive Intelligence: Set up prompts like "Review the Opportunities in this list and identify which ones mention competitors like 'ACME'"

 

For example, imagine creating a custom tool that analyzes all open opportunities for an account, examines related service tickets, and provides insights about customer satisfaction and sales potential - all in one view.

 

Getting Started

If you're an existing customer

These features are now available in your current package. Access them through the updated AI Toolkit interface and new API endpoints.

 

For new customers

Check out SAP CX AI Toolkit on our website, and contact your SAP representative to learn more about these capabilities and discuss next steps.

 

Looking Ahead

This release represents our ongoing commitment to enhancing your business processes with the latest in AI as we continue to develop new features based on your feedback and needs.

 

I'm excited for what we'll deliver together in 2025!

#businessai #sapcx #cxaitoolkit

entry.title: Background Data is updated in Candidate Profile but not updated in Background section in Application
entry.content:
Hello,

There is a KBA that states candidate details have to be completed prior to Recruiter clicking into Application otherwise it will not feed over but when the candidate is asked to complete these details after Recruiter review, it is not updating in the background section of the Application page

In speaking with our support partner, they were suggesting an automation intervention by creating an integration in Integration Center which uses SF backend APIs which can automatically update the Background portlet in Application page however the cost is astronomical.

I wanted to see if any other companies are dealing with this issue and what their workaround is.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Is there any way to sync an all-Page filter with an Input_Control filter?
entry.content:
Scenario:

I have a bar chart with Skill dimension on Page 1. If any skill is selected from this chart, it should jump to Page 2 & filter out the whole of Page 2 with that selected skill. This I've implemented using the SAC Hyperlink option from the Chart Add-ons section.
But Page 2 is not getting filtered. There is an Input_Control filter also present in Page 2 & this page is retaining the value of the Input_Control instead of the SAC Hyperlink filter option. Not getting any data on Page 2 as two filters are colliding.

My possible solution:

I've added another bar chart (will keep this hidden as this is an extra chart) on Page 2 with Skills dimension & removed this chart from the Input_Control. So, the Input_Control will not filter this bar chart. Now, when I filter Page 2 from Page 1 using that hyperlink filter, this bar chart on page 2 is showing data / getting filtered that is coming from Page 1.
Using the below code which I wrote on the onInitialization section of Page 2, I'm able to capture the data from this bar chart in a variable.

var x = Chart_1.getDataSource().getResultSet()[0][Alias.MeasureDimension].formattedValue;

Now I want to push this x value to the Input_Control, so that I can filter Page 2 from Page 1 in this way. I'm unable to write a code for this part. Is this possible to achieve? If yes, could anyone please explain how I can do this?

entry.title: Re: Extending a Standard API - A Guide to Enhancing API_QUALITYNOTIFICATION

entry.title: Re: Document Area difference between DMS and DMS2.
entry.content: Did you find an answer to this 😄

entry.title: Why Does Value Matter in the Customer Experience?
entry.content:
In an era where customers have an abundance of choices, merely showcasing the “features and functions” of a (energy) product, is not enough. Businesses must prioritize delivering value to create meaningful connections with their customers. Understanding why value matters in customer experience is pivotal for companies, including Utilities, striving to stand out, build trust, and ensure the longevity of customer relationships.

Value vs. Features and Functions

I have a long history at SAP in the CX space, starting in the CRM 7 days. And I am guilty to have fallen for the behavior I want to criticize: talking about “features and function”. When discussing software solutions such as SAP CX, it’s imperative to transcend beyond the traditional narrative of features and functions. These describe what the software does, but focusing on the value explains why it matters to the customer. Understanding the essence of value lies in its ability to make conversations more tailored to the specific needs, challenges, and goals of the customer. This personalized approach not only enhances the relevance of the solution but also makes it more compelling.

Treat others like you want to be treated

As a Trusted Advisor, it is important to showcase that you understand the unique situation of each customer reinforces their trust. Customers are increasingly seeking solutions that are personalized rather than generic. They want to feel acknowledged and appreciated, knowing that their specific requirements are being met. By emphasizing value, businesses demonstrate that they are not merely offering a one-size-fits-all solution but are genuinely invested in the success of their customers.

In markets filled with similar offerings, many businesses find themselves competing primarily on price, leading to a commoditized perception. That is already true for energy tariffs but will become a fact even for value added services at some point, if not done correctly. Here lies the opportunity for differentiation: by highlighting the unique value propositions, whether as a standalone solution or as part of bundled offering. This could encompass aspects such as improved energy efficiency, cost savings, increased revenue, or enhanced customer satisfaction.

Differentiate with value

The essence of differentiation through value is in making the purchasing decision clearer and more confident for the customer. It shifts the conversation from being about the product/details to being about the tangible outcomes and benefits that a customer can expect. When a customer perceives the distinct value that a solution provides, it elevates the business above the competition, fostering a clearer and more compelling decision-making process.

When the emphasis is on value, the focus shifts to the customer and their success. This shift is fundamental for building lasting relationships grounded in trust and mutual understanding. Continuously demonstrating and delivering value cultivates strong customer relationships, resulting in improved experiences and enhanced satisfaction. Over time, these relationships build trust and foster loyalty, encouraging customers to become advocates for the brand. A focus on long-term relationship building leads to better customer retention, illustrating that the business is dedicated to the continuous success and growth of its customers.

It is a journey to lead with value

In conclusion, placing value at the forefront of the customer experience offers numerous benefits for businesses. It allows for personalized interactions, clear differentiation in crowded markets, and the capacity to nurture long-term relationships. By emphasizing value, businesses can enhance customer satisfaction, loyalty, and advocacy, ultimately ensuring a sustainable and successful future in an ever-evolving marketplace. Understanding and implementing these principles is key to thriving in today’s competitive environment.

I have the honor and privilege to talk about this topic in a microforum on April 10th at the SAP for Energy & Utilities Conference in Rotterdam. This will only be one topic next to a lot of interesting presentations around Customer Experience at that event. There will be the pre-conference CX workshop,  the SAP Pop-Up Campus offering unique experiences, and of course many insightful customer presentations in the CX track, such as TEAG from Germany.

Make sure to sign up now, to not miss out on such great content for CX and the Utilities industry.

entry.title: No data was released for version Y10 and Consolidation Unit - SAP Group Reporting
entry.content:
Dear all,

 

I'm facing this warning in SAP Group Reporting - Data Monitor for the task "Release from Universal Journal". 

GL ACCOUNTS - FS ITEMS  are mapped

Assign Mapping done

Consolidation Unit is confiugured to read from Universal Journal

Version Y10 is configured in this way:

What could be the problem? how Can I solve this? I have the same problem with the other consolidation units and task Balance Carryforward ended successfully with No Data. 

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: B2B Integration ANSIX12 Inbound 997
entry.content: Hi all, Do we have a solution for this, i am facing the same, request for your valuable suggestions

entry.title: Re: SAP Intelligence - Delete processes, but that the pip...
entry.content:
Hello @CP_PL ,

thanks for your request! My name is Ascha and I am part of the virtual Customer Success Team at SAP Signavio.

To reduce the cases, you would need to:
Delete source data by going to Process Data Pipeline → Source data → Select one of all tables then "Delete only extracted data and keep the tables".
User guide:
https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-intelligence/user-guide/data-deletion-standard-extraction
Remove delta/Partition strategy configuration if you had any.

Configuration phase to configure following:
Set new SQL filter.
SQL filter: this is where you can define a filter that would result in reduced amount of cases: https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-intelligence/user-guide/refine-extraction-with-sql-filters
Then you can setup the initial partition strategy and then extract the initial load.
Once the extraction of all the tables that require data reduction for is complete, you can proceed with the T&L so that the reduced data set overwrites the existing dataset in the process.

Hope this help!

If you need further assistance, please let us know. 

Kind regards

Ascha ( as part of the virtual Customer Success Team)

entry.title: Cant download history and processor information in verify journal entry app in S4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
We need your help to know the process how to get extract of history and processor information in verify journal entry app in public cloud. Verify journal entry app display history and processor detials on main screen but when we are trying to download we cant see this field in downloaded file.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Question: How to restrict access to EnableNow in rega...
entry.content: What you can try is to add these parameters to the SAP Companion settings in SFSF. enableExternalUsage=true;externalUsageTimeout=<time in seconds required for employees to complete the SSO authentication> This trick prevents the failed authentication popup from appearing if the SSO authentication against the IdP fails in the background. The parameters were designed for external content consumption scenarios via SAP Companion, but they have also proven useful as workarounds for cases like yours.

entry.title: Re: Make Manadatory Field in Create Incoming Invoices(FB60)
entry.content:
Did you already look at app Manage Substitution/Validation Rules?

  • Online help - Create Validation Rules

entry.title: Determine number of times each HANA view has been executed, and by whom?
entry.content: I need to know the number of times each view in my HANA system has been executed over the last few months. I also need to know which users have executed those views. Is that possible? Does auditing need to be set up to obtain those metrics? If so, can someone direct me to practical examples? Best Practices and Recommendations for Creating Audit Policies is interesting but none of the examples seems to apply to what I'm trying to accomplish.

entry.title: Re: Streamline Data Migration from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA: Selective Data Transition with Time-Slice
entry.content:
Thanks @matthiasstegmue 

Are there plans to include SAP Business Transformation Center's Selective Data Transition into the Early SAP Adopter Program ? The feature and its enhancements are quite recents, and would reassure both Partners and Customers having SAP helping them in case of unexpected issues

Also have there been many customers who actually went Live, in Production on S/4HANA using  SAP Business Transformation Center's Selective Data Transition  ?

entry.title: Re: Migration of Interview Scheduling using Microsoft Outlook Integration to Work Tech

I reposted your Q to the SuccessFactors Recruiting User Group. Hopefully we'll get better response there! 🙂

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-successfactors-recruiting-user-group/repost-from-mfpabon-challenges-migrating-interview-scheduling-to-work-tech/m-p/14022842#M982

Thanks!
Tara

entry.title: Re: Is it mandatory to have a partner for a lift and shif...
entry.content: If you are asking this question it means that you definitely need a Partner.

entry.title: DCD1 condition type is getting determined for US company codes
entry.content:
Hello Team,

 

DCD1 ( Cash Discount ) condition type is getting determined for US company codes based on the payment maintained in the preliminary billing document or the payment terms in the customer master.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain for Transportation Management: Freight Cost Allocation to Material Valu
entry.content:
Hi Atul

Thank you for a good blog post. I do have a couple of questions regarding the process you describe at the beginning of the blog. The process ends with this step:

In parallel (with integration to the SAP S/4HANA Settlement Management integration below accounting entries are also created (provided the setup is correctly done):

Debit Material

Credit Freight Clearing

What happens to the Credit items on the Freight Clearing account? Are there further steps in the process creating a Debit entry on the Freight Clearing account. Or will it just accumulate over time. The invoice from the carrier seems to be posted on the GR/IR account used for material reciepts and invoices. So the invoice is clearing the freight service offset posting on the GR/IR account.

And what is the purpose of the service acceptance document where you debit a Freight cost account?
In your example the cost are also posted to the stock accounts in your balance sheet. So it seems to be double up.

Best regards

Claus Møller Jensen

entry.title: Re: DCD1 condition type is getting determined for US company codes
entry.content: DCD1 ( Cash Discount ) condition type is not getting determined for US company codes based on the payment maintained in the preliminary billing document or the payment terms in the customer master.

entry.title: Re: Microsoft Outlook and Teams Integration for Interview Scheduling
entry.content:
Hi @mfpabon,

Niel at SAP Support told me just today on my ticket that no, you do not need different credentials for Preview vs Production.

I have reached out internally about the first inquiry since I can't find information about it in our docs including implementation guide. I already received an [answer] and confirmed that using the same MS exchange credentials in your Preview and Production should not cause a problem in your configuration. Both should work fine, nevertheless, I think the consequence will be the possibility of your employees getting emails from your Preview instance.

Hope that helps!
Tara

entry.title: Projected stock is not being generated within the Location Product Substitution Set-up
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I am encountering an issue with the location product substitution functionality in SAP IBP and would appreciate your insights.


Setup details:
Infinite heuristic
Discontinuation with use-up (including SOFTSTOP and HARDSTOP)
When I run an application job, the substitution behaves correctly in most aspects:
When there is no stock for the old product, the system stops planning for it (projected stock becomes empty from that period onwards).
The Location-Product-Substitution Supply key figure for the new product are filled with the demand from the old product.


The issue:
Projected stock at the new product is not being generated.


If there is stock on hand for the new product, it gets consumed over several months, and I can see the projected stock accordingly. However, once the stock is used-up, projected stock remains empty.
The same happens if there is no initial stock on hand for the new product—projected stock just is not being generated.


Does anyone know why this might be happening and how to adjust the system to generate projected stock correctly? Am I missing a configuration or master data setup?


I’ve attached screenshots for reference.

Thanks a lot in advance for your help!

entry.title: how to restrict serial numbers at plant level in ewm
entry.content:
Hi All,

Our requirement is, two plants are linked to one EWM warehouse(MNCH). Material ABCD123 is available in two plants (1000 and 2000) with different serial numbers in the warehouse. Delivery is created for 1000 plant. Expectation is, while picking user scans the serial numbers and system should allow to pick serial number belongs to 1000 plant only but not 2000. Now it is allowing to pick the serial numbers from both the plant. How to restrict the serial numbers to pick only from that particular plant.

Please suggest.

Thanks in advance!

Regards,

Sachin.

entry.title: Re: Automatic creation for Purchase Requisitions for Service
entry.content:
Hi @Wesley89,
Could you please explain more about the requirement?
In-general as per the PR requirement:
If it is for one time - we may go for PO creation by finding relevant vendor.
If it is for multiple times - we may go for contract/scheduling agreement creation.


If the PR to be created on monthly basis - get it create a custom program with technical team(ABAP) with reference to a custom table (where PR relevant data can be entered).
Schedule that PR custom program as a background with monthly period.

entry.title: How to Mass Delete Planned Orders for Multiple Materials at one time
entry.content: I am working in SAP RP1 and trying to find a way to delete multiple Planned orders for multiple materials at one time.  Any suggestions?

Also how the transactional data will be transferred from S4Hana Core (On Premise) to TM (S4Hana private cloud).

Regards

entry.title: fill Component list from maintenance plans work orders
entry.content:
Hello all,

I Need some help Please?

I am looking for a way to be able to have my components tab already filled out with parts required to completed the task for all of my maintenance work orders created by a certain maintenance plan.

Many Thanks!

entry.title: Store file content into a Z table
entry.content:
Hello,

One of our external partner is sending a flat file into our SAP system's folder, our custom prog is reading the file and creating the POs in SAP, well, but we want to make sure that the external partner should not send the same file again, want to avoid duplicating. External Partner is not accepting to provide any kind of Identifier or unique ID or GUID or time stamp inside the file so that our custom prog validate the duplication, pls. let us know how to avoid the duplication or processing the same file again? What about our custom prog will store all the file content in a Z table after converting it into binary data (like SAP stores long texts) so every single time our custom prog will validate current file content with stored content in the Z table, our system is unicode enabled / ECC?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: How to implement "readonly : update" for draft instances
entry.content:
Hi all,

IMHO this is yet another example for the chatty nature of RAP.  RAP should IMHO more follow a convention over configuration approach.

What else could be ment by default with "field ( readonly : update ) AgencyId;" for a draft enabled entity than editable for a freshly created draft entry until the draft is persisted and readonly thereafter?

While it is of cource nice that we can achieve this with 20 lines of code (the 39 lines above contain some empty lines and code could be a bit more dense through the use of VALUE constructors), I would want this without any additional line...

Regards,

  Wolfgang

entry.title: Hi All - Where can i find a list of training and Certification courses for Business Users?
Re: SAP CPQ - Bring interactive, real-time 3D Product Configuration from Expivi
entry.content:
@MehmetErcomert 

There are no pre-requisities for integrating with Expivi with SAP CPQ. you will have to design with RD templates according to your need. 3D Configurator can run without SAP CommerceCloud

entry.title: The email address control does not recognise an incorrect format as an error - Career site - Rec
entry.content:
The email address control does not recognize an incorrect format as an error. The client has a need of covering the situation when an email does not have the correct format - show the appropriate error message (like it works to First name and Last name fields).

We need it to cover WCAG-complaince issues

SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting

entry.title: Seeking Eclipse ADT Equivalent to SE37's "Save Parameters as Test Data" Feature
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm currently working with Eclipse ADT and am missing a feature that was available in the older SE37 transaction (ABAP Workbench).

Specifically, I'm looking for a way to save parameter values from an ABAP program or function module as test data. This functionality allowed me to easily create predefined sets of input parameters for testing purposes.

Does anyone know of any equivalent features or plugins within Eclipse ADT that can achieve this? Any suggestions or workarounds would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay - Individual Goal rating from Performanc...
entry.content:
Hi @glequell 

Yes both are possible. The integration is always from PM to VP and never from GM to VP directly so in order to retrieve a goal and its "achievement %" field into VP you must first pull the goal into a performance form and then set map VP to retrieve it from the right section of the PM form as below:

 

 

 

Rating from the performance form: 

(from this blog)

 

Rating from the Goal "achievement" field: 

(From this blog)

 

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Details: com.sap.aii.utilxi.misc.api.BaseRuntimeException; Character reference "&#, BaseRuntimeExce
entry.content:
HI Experts,

 

I do have a Scenario RFC to REST , when data is been pushed from SAP to PO System , i am facing error in PO monitoring 

 

Error :  Details: com.sap.aii.utilxi.misc.api.BaseRuntimeException; Character reference "&#, BaseRuntimeException: Character reference "&#, BaseRuntimeException: Character reference "&#, org.xml.sax.SAXParseException; lineNumber: 1; columnNumber: 201; Character reference "&#

However this the data that sent from SAP for these fields

    "remark": "Tested from Postman",

    "rejectReason": "Not rejected Test Postman"

 

NOTE : Total we have 8 fields, data is in payload is displaying as expected .Only for the above mentioned fields , payload is showing as

 


<REJECTREASON>N�o�t� �r�e�j�e�c�t�e�d� �T�e�s�t� �P�o�s�t�m�a�n</REJECTREASON>
<REMARK>T�e�s�t�e�d� �f�r�o�m� �P�o�s�t�m�a�n</REMARK>

 

 

Can someone please help me on this .

entry.title: Re: I cannot see the list of which folders and stories a...
entry.content:
Hi @AtillaCemYildizJ,

1. If you want check which user have access to folders story or model you can check by Go in System>Overview>Security 

And select object you want check either it's model,story, folder, application

 2. If you want to check user role 

Go to Security> User> role are mentioned in front of user name 

3. You can check user activity as well through security tab 

 

Kindly upvote/accept solution if you find it helpful

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Feature Request: Flow Function for Opening Apple Subs...
entry.content:
@marihirvi  Is there any chance that SAP Build App will gain this feature in the future?

Thanks in advance for your feedback!

entry.title: Re: 2H 2024 Release - Automatic import of OCN items
entry.content: I don't have a confirmed answer but am also interested in the solution. We are in the same situation - we are implementing LinkedIn Learning OCN, and only want to import the items in our supported locales (currently only English).

entry.title: Re: Building a Serial Number Tracker Report for Production Materials in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

In case anyone encounters this post, there is a new Fiori App Serial Number History / F7368

Which has comparable functionality and alleviates the need for a custom report.

entry.title: NF-e Technical Note 2018.005 - Technical Responsible
entry.content:
Part of the NT 2018 - 005 v1.04 we are implementing the changes for SEFAZ PR which is to inform CSRT and hashCSRT.
According to SAP on OSS note 2780735, SAP will provide CSRT only for customer with is using Document Report and Compliance(DRC cloud solution). What about the old GRC NFE 10.0? (SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil 10.0). SAP should provide CSRT as well or should we maintain our own CNPJ as solution provider? 

Some of you have a similiar issue using GRC NFe ?

entry.title: Re: MEASUREMENT POINT MAINTENANCE PLAN DUE BASED ON LAST...
entry.content:
Try this and let me know if it works out for you:

  1. Set the shift factors on the maintenance plan.
  2. The maintenance plan should be setup to create a PM order
  3. Call the maintenance plan schedule
  4. While TECO the order, there is a popup to enter Reference Date and time. There is also a button to create measurement documents. Enter the relevant measurement.
  5. Review the maintenance plan schedule. It should be adjusted based on the shift factor and the newly created measurement document.

entry.title: Re: Restrict Delete Facts in Modeler but allow user to pl...
entry.content:
Hi @BhaktiBagkar,

You can do this by Custom Access 

1. Go to model

2. Click on Share model option 

3. Select Custom options 

4. Select Edit only as shown below & save.

 

Kindly upvote if you find it helpful.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Consuming CDS View Entities Using ODBC-Based Client Tools
entry.content:
Dear @Frank-Martin ,

we try to replicate the concept but with an S//4HANA On Premise system. All is good until we try to access the SQL Services (both custom and /sap/bc/sql/sql1/sap/s_privileged as described in the documentation).

We could not find any service in SICF under this path /sap/bc/sql/sql1/ and we cannot access our Z SQL service neither also we could see SQL services in SE80. It is like the services do not generate any entry in SICF.

Are we missing a step ? 

Many thanks !

SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database  SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: SAP IBP Keyfigure value for last day of the month
entry.content:
Hi experts!

 

I'd like to set a keyfigure calculation to define a value for the last day of the month.

Let's say:

If 'last day of the month', '1', '2'.

Is there any condition to test the period as last day of the month?

 

Thanks a lot!

Best regards!

 

Victor

entry.title: Re: Splitting Strings in SuccessFactors Business Rule
entry.content: Really cool, thanks for sharing !

entry.title: Re: Use of elements not permitted in CDS view Data Defini...
entry.content:
Hi @mpredolim 

I found this SAP note https://me.sap.com/notes/3573372/E There SAP explains this is a known bug. The note mentions bug will be fixed on March 1st-2nd. It also suggests a workaround.

In my case, I noticed I can read the tables using an Abap class + Eclipse Debugger.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Creating an L3-L8 Approval Hierarchy
entry.content:
Hi @Annie3 

Sorry I was on parental leave when you posted this question and unfortunately my colleagues didn't pick up on it.

We usually don't recommend adding too many compensation forms route map steps (we recommend max 2 manager steps). If you want additional approvers we recommend using the Executive review based on RBP and leverage the new 2H 2024 "Exec review based on route step" feature to trigger permissions automatically based on route steps. 

Thanks a lot !

Xavier

entry.title: Re: SAP TM in private cloud
entry.content: Hi, Did you get answer of this

entry.content:
Hi @DiegoValdivia. Thanks for the input.

I already saw this note and for me the only solution is wait until SAP fix the issue.

Regards,
Mauricio Predolim

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Threshold Visualization Issue
entry.content: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/applying-thresholds-on-sac-charts-tables-and-addressing-common-issues/ba-p/13954344

entry.title: Advanced intercompany processes & the concept of transactional consolidation
entry.content:
The first part of the blog series introduced the concept of parallel accounting and more specifically “Universal Parallel Accounting”. I'd like to bring it to your attention here: From Parallel Accounting to Universal Parallel Accounting

We focused on a specific use case involving parallel accounting from the group's perspective, where parts of the production process are carried out in different countries or legal entities to achieve economies of scale. When analyzed from the perspective of the company buying or selling the intermediate (finished) product, it is simply an intercompany sale/purchase process with a mark-up added to the product. However, from the Group/HQ’s perspective, there are two additional requirements: obtaining a detailed cost component breakdown, even after the product has crossed borders, changed material codes, or had value added to it; and ensuring a margin-free valuation of the final product.

A quick recap of the example used in the first blog with valuation from legal and group perspectives:

In this second blog, we'll explore how advancements in logistics processes, particularly the improvements in advanced intercompany processes, affect "Finance." There are several excellent articles detailing "Advanced intercompany flows," one of which I highly recommend: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. In this post, my focus will primarily be on the financial implications.

Referring to our example: there are two intercompany processes at play:

  1. The first part of the production process occurs at Plant 1010 in Germany. The partially processed material (ZPREETHA_FG_01) is then transferred to Plant 1710 in the USA for further processing, resulting in the transformation to ZPREETHA_FG_02. From a logistics standpoint, this can be efficiently managed using the new and improved "Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer" process within SAP S/4HANA.
  2. The German affiliate sells the finished product to a customer in the US. Since the material is produced by the US affiliate at Plant 1710, the plant 1710 directly delivers the product to the customer. Some customers refer to this as a "triangular flow", while in SAP terminology, this is known as the new and improved "Advanced Intercompany Sales process."

I will focus on the "Advanced Intercompany Sales Process" and explain the concept of transactional consolidation by analyzing the accounting impacts. I will also highlight how this process addresses the needs of both plant controllers and group controllers.

Let’s proceed step by step:

  1. Sales to Final Customer:

Legal entity 1010 in Germany creates a sales order for a customer in the US, with plant 1710 in the US designated as the delivering plant. Technically, the sales area of the selling plant must have access to delivering plant 1710 to facilitate deliveries from an affiliated company. A new field, the transit plant, is used to define the plant for valuated stock in transit postings within the sales order.

Intercompany sales and purchase orders are automatically generated when the sales order to the final customer is saved. This is accomplished through the Value Chain Monitoring framework. To trigger the automatic creation of intercompany documents (both purchase orders and sales orders), the sales order type and item category must be configured to support the advanced intercompany process (e.g., sales order type = OR and item category = TAN).

Additionally, it is necessary to configure the sales order type and item category for intercompany sales orders, as well as the purchase order type for intercompany transactions. Furthermore, plants and company codes must be set up as business partners (ship-to, payer), which we will see later when analyzing the postings.

2. Delivery to Final Customer:

The outbound delivery is created for the final customer and the goods are shipped from the plant 1710 in the US. Upon posting the goods issue from the outbound delivery document, the following goods movement records are automatically generated:

  • From the delivering company side:
    • Goods transfer from storage location to valuated stock in transit (GIOD)
    • Goods issue from valuated stock in transit (GIFV)
  • From the selling company side:
    • Goods receipt into storage location (GRTS)
    • Goods transfer from storage location to valuated stock in transit (GIFS)
    • Goods issue from valuated stock in transit (GITC)

These goods movements are automatically posted based on two new fields in the delivery document: the internal and external transfer of control dates. The Value Chain Monitoring Framework executes these automatic goods movements via scheduled jobs according to these dates.

Let’s now examine the accounting scheme for each of the accounting documents.

Goods transfer from storage location to valuated stock in transit (Delivering Company):

As the first step, a special stock indicator is assigned to the stock, enabling the transfer of goods to valuated stock in transit.

In the leading ledger 0L, the posting is based on legal valuation with an intercompany mark-up:

In the group ledger 4G, the posting is based on the group valuation without any intercompany mark-up:

Goods issue from valuated stock in transit (Delivering Company):

The second goods movement represents the cost of sales posting from the US affiliate to the German affiliate. 

In the leading ledger 0L, the posting is based on legal valuation with an intercompany mark-up:

Additionally, in the market segment view, the German affiliate is identified as both the sold-to and bill-to party. However, the final customer in the US is designated as the ship-to party.

In the group ledger 4G, the posting is based on the group valuation without any intercompany mark-up.

In addition to this margin-free valuation in the group ledger 4G, a reversal of the Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) is also posted, with the “Group Valuation Clearing Account” serving as the offsetting account. This reversal posting sets the stage for what we call as “transactional consolidation”.

Goods Receipt into storage location (Selling Company):

This document represents the Goods receipt posting in the selling company, the German affiliate 1010.

In the leading ledger 0L, the posting is based on legal valuation with an intercompany mark-up:

The price difference of 66,6 $ is due to currency translation. This is demonstrated below: the exchange rate type M is used for average rate conversion, while exchange rate type P is utilized for planning or standard cost calculation."

In the group ledger 4G, the posting is based on the group valuation without any intercompany mark-up.

The amounts in the Accounts Receivable (AR) and Accounts Payable (AP) accounts are identical, with the values in the Price Difference (PRD) account attributable to differences in exchange rates.

Goods transfer from storage location to valuated stock in transit:

A special stock indicator is then assigned to the stock, enabling the transfer of goods to valuated stock in transit.

In the leading ledger 0L, the posting is based on legal valuation with an intercompany mark-up:

In the group ledger 4G, the posting is based on the group valuation without any intercompany mark-up:

Goods issue from valuated stock in transit:

This document represents the Goods issue posting in the selling company, the German affiliate 1010.

In the leading ledger 0L, the posting is based on legal valuation with an intercompany mark-up:

Additionally, in the market segment view, the final customer in the US is identified as the sold-to, bill-to and ship-to party for the German affiliate.

In the group ledger 4G, the posting is based on the group valuation without any intercompany mark-up.

From the perspective of the German affiliate, the transaction is as a direct material purchase. Consequently, the detailed cost component breakdown should reflect this as a “direct material” purchase. This is shown in the posting in the leading ledger 0L for the COGS split.

However, from the group perspective, we will have a detailed breakdown of the cost components, excluding any intercompany margins. This can be seen in the COGS split posting in the group ledger 4G

This is how SAP addresses the needs of a corporate or group controller who needs to analyze a detailed cost component breakdown without any intercompany mark-up, even after the product has crossed borders, changed material codes, or had value added to it.

3. Invoice to Final Customer & Invoice to intercompany customer:

After generating the outbound delivery, the final customer invoice can be created. There is no prescribed order for this process: the final customer invoice and the intercompany sales invoice can be created simultaneously and independently of each other.

When the intercompany sales invoice is created, it automatically generates the intercompany vendor invoice through the Value Chain Monitoring Framework.

Let’s now examine the accounting scheme for each of the accounting documents.

Invoice to Final customer from the German Affiliate:

Posting in the leading ledger 0L:

In the market segment view, the final customer in the US is identified as the sold-to, bill-to and ship-to party for the German affiliate.

Posting in the group ledger 4G:

Intercompany Customer Invoice:

Posting in the leading ledger 0L: (based on the valuation in the material master)

In the market segment view, the German affiliate is identified as both the sold-to and bill-to party. However, the final customer in the US is designated as the ship-to party, similar to what we have seen in the Goods Issue document

Posting in the group ledger 4G:

When we posted the Cost of sales, there was a reversal of the amounts in COGS account with “Group Valuation Clearing Account” being the offsetting account. Similarly, the intercompany revenue is also reversed in the intercompany customer invoice with the “Group Valuation Clearing Account” serving as the offsetting accounting. This process effectively reverses both intercompany COGS and intercompany Sales, resulting in an inherent “transactional consolidation” within our Profit and Loss (P&L) statement.

When discussing about "transactional consolidation" in this blog, I primarily focused on the Profit & Loss (P&L) statement. These concepts hold significant value for controllers and CFOs who require a real-time view of their sales and margins, rather than waiting until the end of the period. This also aligns with our "continuous accounting" narrative in our SAP S/4HANA pitch for Finance.

While the blog centers on management accounting, consolidation from a statutory perspective involves additional complexities, such as understanding the ownership structure and consolidating figures accordingly. This is where our consolidation solution, "Group Reporting," comes into play.

To learn more about this, I highly recommend the following blogs:

Tight Accounting Integration:

https://blogs.sap.com/2022/01/31/sap-s-4hana-cloud-for-group-reporting-2202-is-live/

Value Chain Insight and Integration Group Reporting:

https://blogs.sap.com/2022/10/10/insights-into-value-chain/

And finally, in the third part of the blog series, I shall discuss how Intercompany Matching and Reconciliation (ICMR) facilitates the matching and reconciliation of intercompany balances before consolidating figures in Group Reporting. I will demonstrate how our built-in solution streamlines this process, eliminating the need for complex Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) procedures. This integrated approach ensures that any latencies associated with intercompany transactions are effectively eliminated.
